summary: Crazy Fingers > Terrapin magic, soaring Bird Song and Dark Star jam
blurb: The show shines with a locked‑in Crazy Fingers → Terrapin Station sequence and a soaring Bird Song climax. Jerry’s vocals on Terrapin are spot‑on, while the second set delivers an unstoppable Dark Star Jam and tight drum work.
review: Richfield ’91 is a standout entry in the Hornsby era, opening strong with Feel Like A Stranger and Bertha before building into a soaring Bird Song that sets the tone for a high‑energy night. The second set kicks off with Victim Or The Crime but truly ignites on Crazy Fingers, where Jerry’s guitar is described as “locked‑in” and his vocal delivery on Terrapin Station is beautiful, backed by perfect guitar tone and lyrical precision. Bruce Hornsby and Vince Welnick provide lush keyboard textures, highlighted by a grand piano solo that adds a Munsters‑theme flavor to the warm‑up jam. The rhythm section – Mickey and Bill – drives an extended Terrapin jam and later a Dark Star Jam that never loses its momentum, leading into a fiery All Along The Watchtower before winding down with Stella Blue and Around & Around. Fans consistently cite this show as one of their top three performances, and the Miller‑remastered soundboard capture showcases the band at a creative peak.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six recordings are marked positive, with multiple reviewers praising the same moments – Crazy Fingers → Terrapin Station, Bird Song climax, and Dark Star Jam – indicating strong consensus of exceptional playing across the board.

best_recording.reason: Miller‑remastered soundboard with clear, well‑balanced mix; slight hum is negligible and it captures the full energy of the performance.

key_highlights: Unforgettable Crazy Fingers → Terrapin Station sequence with locked‑in Jerry.
Soaring Bird Song climax that drives first‑set momentum.
Extended second‑set featuring a Dark Star Jam, All Along The Watchtower and tight drum work.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers a locked‑in performance on Crazy Fingers, beautiful vocals and perfect guitar tone on Terrapin Station, and nails the lyrics throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Provides his steady bass foundation; highlighted by the classic "We Want Phil" chant before Just Like Tom Thumb's Blues.
band_performance.Bob: His presence is noted as appreciated, adding solid rhythm guitar support.
band_performance.Brent: Bruce Hornsby and Vince Welnick shine with lush keyboards; a standout piano solo adds flavor, and their interplay creates an ambient backdrop without overwhelming the mix.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums are tight and drive the extended Terrapin jam and Dark Star Jam with precision.
band_performance.Billy: Partners Mickey in delivering powerful, cohesive percussion that propels the second set.
